div1.entryFree:
Belch, vb., 1) absol. to vomit: the --ing whale, Troil. V, 5, 23. Per. III, 1, 63. 'tis a good constraint of fortune it (the sea) --es upon us, Per. III, 2, 55. thy food is such as hath been --ed on by infected lungs, IV, 6, 179.
2) trans. to vomit forth: my panting bulk which almost burst to b. it in the sea, R3 I, 4, 41. when they are full, they b. us, Oth. III, 4, 106. the bitterness of it I now b. from my heart, Cymb. III, 5, 137. -- to belch up, in the same sense: Tp. III, 3, 56.
